title: Pasqal and MegazoneCloud Sign MoU for Neutral-Atom Hardware Deployment in South Korea - Quantum Computing Report
gist: Neutral-atom vendor Pasqal signed a non-binding MoU with South Korea's MegazoneCloud to distribute its QPU hardware and QSDKs via managed cloud plus on-premises installs, targeting finance, logistics, biotech and manufacturing—while Pasqal continues its Nasdaq listing via SPAC Bleichroeder Acquisition Corp. II (BBCQ).

insights.claim: Pasqal is using a cloud managed-service partner (MegazoneCloud) as its distribution channel into South Korea, connecting its full-stack backends and QSDKs to regional cloud environments rather than selling hardware direct.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Cloud-MSP partnerships are Pasqal's low-capex route to national enterprise markets; watch for similar regional MoUs.
insights.quote: the domestic distribution of Pasqal’s hardware layers via MegazoneCloud’s managed cloud service infrastructure

insights.claim: The deal follows a three-stage model—cloud access, use-case workshops, then on-premises QPU installs integrated into regional HPC for hybrid classical-quantum acceleration—mapping a pilot-to-production adoption funnel.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: The staged model shows how quantum vendors intend to convert pilots into recurring on-prem/HPC revenue.
insights.quote: a structured pathway to transition from isolated quantum pilot projects to stable, hardware-backed cloud production systems
insights.locator: By leveraging MegazoneCloud’s cloud engineering infrastructure

insights.claim: The MoU targets financial services, logistics optimization, biotechnology, and manufacturing as priority verticals for neutral-atom deployment in South Korea.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Identifies which enterprise verticals Pasqal is prioritizing for early commercial traction.
insights.locator: Second, the entities will establish technical design workshops

insights.claim: Pasqal is pursuing a Nasdaq public listing via a business combination with SPAC Bleichroeder Acquisition Corp. II (Nasdaq: BBCQ), adding a near-term liquidity/capital event to its commercialization push.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: A tradable ticker (via BBCQ) gives investors a public neutral-atom pure-play once the SPAC combination closes.
insights.quote: through a business combination with the special purpose acquisition company Bleichroeder Acquisition Corp. II
insights.locator: Concurrently, Pasqal continues its public listing track
